description: Detection accuracy across all experimental runs with normal distribution fit and 95% confidence interval.
analysis: Across 10 independent runs (each 20 trials, 15 scored), the mean detection accuracy is 60.7% (SD=0.12). The 95% confidence interval is [52.1%, 69.2%], well above the random baseline of 10%. A one-sample t-test against the random baseline yields t(9)=13.41, p=0.0000, confirming that detection accuracy is significantly above chance.
